A.reason: The between-sweep blind window is real and the branch-advance-triggered micro-sweep closes exactly that window, but its own cited numbers (13 issues filed vs an open count of 1→2, severity stripped) show a nearer fire — the ledger tear — that A relegates to a secondary rule instead of the diagnosis.

G.reason: The only lane whose risk is already burning on the page (13 findings/3 HIGH vs 1 open unspecified is a self-contradiction in its own vitals), and the state-based ledger-vs-sweep diff fires today on two of its four conditions while explicitly surviving the observation-gaps that would blind an event-triggered guard.

F.reason: Chained-pulse-plus-null-vitals is a real exposure and the emit-time verifier covers all three named manifestations (broken link, stale pin, silent heart) with a clear refuse-to-broadcast action, docked slightly for treating any pinned-pillar HEAD advance as drift, which conflates a content pin with a branch freeze.

note: The most correct diagnosis in the field is Lane G's: it is the only lane whose named risk is proven by its own evidence at judging time — the sweep ledger and issue ledger visibly contradict each other on the page, so its guard would fire today rather than on a hypothesized future event.
